LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER, OR ALTERNATIVE ORDER SHORTENING TIME FOR HEARING ON PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A PROPOSED STIPULATION AND ORDER. THE PROPOSED STIPULATION AND ORDER SHOULD BE DELIVERED TO THE COURT BY FEBRUARY 20, 2009. IF COUNSEL, CANNOT STIPULATE AN AGREEMENT, THEN THE COURT'S RULING WILL BE THAT NEITHER SIDE IS TO TOUCH OR ALTER THE TREE IN ANY MANNER. COUNSEL TO PROVIDE ORDER. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: CONNIE J. PARCHMAN, CSR #6137

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT VINOD KRIPALANI, and, ASYA KRIPALANI MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. The complaint only contains conclusory allegations but no supporting facts for the punitive damages claim. Cross-complainant must plead specific facts (events, dates, etc.) supporting malice, oppression, fraud, or despicable conduct. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction, Temporary Restraining Order, Or Alternative Order Shortening Time For Hearing On Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary injunction, temporary restraining order, or alternative Order shortening time for hearing on preliminary injunction. Appearances: Stephen L. Porter, Esq. of Whitehead & Porter LLP, (415) 781-6070; appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Richard M. McNeely, Esq. of McNamara, Dodge, Ney, Beatty, Slattery, Pfalzer, Borges & Brothers LLP, (925) 939-5330, appearing for Defendants, Paul Martin and Sharon Yost, and opposing party. Argued and the Court vacated its tentative ruling, and rules as follows: counsel are to meet and confer regarding a proposed stipulation and Order. The proposed stipulation and order should be delivered to the Court by February 20, 2009. If counsel, cannot stipulate an agreement, then the Court's ruling will be that neither side is to touch or alter the tree in any manner. Counsel to provide Order. (302/PHA/X-LT) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Court Reporter: Connie J. Parchman, CSR 6137
